title: Display of medical image data
abstract: A system and method are provided for display of medical image data, with the display of the medical image data being determined on the basis of schematic image data of a schematic representation of an anatomical structure. The schematic representation may provide a particular view of the anatomical structure. The type of anatomical structure and the view of the anatomical structure provided by the schematic representation may be determined based on one or more image features in the schematic image data. The view may be characterized as a geometrically-defined perspective at which the anatomical structure is shown in the schematic representation. An output image may be generated showing the anatomical structure in the medical image data in accordance with said determined geometrically-defined perspective. A user may thus be provided with a display of medical image data which is easier to interpret having considered said schematic representation.
claims:
Claims 1. A system (100) for display of medical image data, comprising: an input interface (120) for accessing first image data (030) comprising a schematic representation (210, 212) of an anatomical structure, the schematic representation providing a particular view of the anatomical structure; a memory (160) comprising instruction data representing a set of instructions; a processor (140) configured to communicate with the input interface and the memory and to execute the set of instructions, wherein the set of instructions, when executed by the processor, cause the processor to: analyze the first image data to determine i) a type of anatomical structure and ii) the view of the anatomical structure provided by the schematic representation, based on one or more image features in the first image data, the view being characterized as a geometrically-defined perspective at which the anatomical structure is shown in the schematic representation; via the input interface, access second image data (032) being volumetric image data acquired of a patient, the second image data comprising the type of anatomical structure; and generate an output image (320) showing the anatomical structure in the second image data in accordance with said geometrically-defined perspective. 2. The system (100) according to claim 1, wherein the schematic representation (210, 212) of the anatomical structure comprises an annotation (220), and wherein the set of instructions cause the processor (140) to: detect an anatomical location of the annotation in the schematic representation of the anatomical structure; and based on said detected anatomical location, annotate (330) the output image (320) in correspondence with the annotation in the schematic representation. 3. The system (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the set of instructions cause the processor (140) to determine the type of anatomical structure and/or the view of the anatomical structure by: 

 via the input interface (120), accessing a database comprising i) image data of a set of schematic representations of anatomical structures and ii) metadata indicating for each schematic representation the type of anatomical structure and/or the view of the anatomical structure provided by the respective schematic representation; and comparing the first image data (030) to the image data of each, or a subset of the set of schematic representations. 4. The system (100) according to claim 3, wherein said comparing comprises: registering the first image data (030) to the image data of each, or the subset of the set of schematic representations, and determining a correspondence of said registered image data. 5. The system (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the set of instructions cause the processor (140) to determine the type of anatomical structure and/or the view of the anatomical structure provided by the schematic representation (210, 212) by applying an image classification technique to the first image data (030). 6. The system (100) according to claim 5, wherein the image classification technique is based on machine learning. 7. The system (100) according to claim 6, wherein the image classification technique is trained to identify a view provided by the schematic representation by: accessing a 3D model (400) of the type of anatomical structure, such as a segmentation model for segmenting the type of anatomical structure; generating projections of the 3D model from different viewpoints, thereby obtaining a set of 2D images; using the 2D images and data characterizing the different viewpoints as training input to the image classification technique. 8. The system (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the first image data (030) comprises an image-based code indicative of the type of anatomical part and/or the view of the anatomical structure provided by the schematic representation, wherein the set of instructions cause the processor (140) to analyze the first image data to identify the image- based code. 9. The system (100) according to any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the set of instructions cause the processor (140) to further characterize the view as a type of view provided by the schematic representation, such as a cross-sectional or exterior view, and to generate the output image (320) in accordance with the type of view. 10. The system (100) according to claim 1, wherein the set of instructions cause the processor (140) to generate the output image (320) using at least one: multi-planar reformatting, intensity projection and volume rendering. 11. The system (100) according to anyone of claims 1 to 10, wherein the set of instructions cause the processor (140) to: via the input interface (120), access model data defining a segmentation model (400) for segmenting the type of anatomical structure, the segmentation model comprising a set of anatomical landmarks; apply the segmentation model to the second image data (032), thereby obtaining a patient-adapted segmentation model comprising a patient-adapted set of anatomical landmarks; and determine the view to be shown of the anatomical structure in the output image based on the patient-adapted set of anatomical landmarks. 12. The system (100) according to any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the second image data (032) comprises a time-series of images representing an anatomical cycle of the anatomical structure, wherein set of instructions cause the processor (140) to: determine which part of the anatomical cycle is shown by the view of the anatomical structure in the schematic representation (210, 212); and generate the output image to show the anatomical structure in the second image data in accordance with said determined part of the anatomical cycle. 13. A workstation or imaging apparatus comprising the system (100) according to any one of claims 1 to 12. 14. A computer-implemented method (500) for display of medical image data, comprising: 

 accessing (510) first image data comprising a schematic representation of an anatomical structure, the schematic representation providing a particular view of the anatomical structure; analyzing (520) the first image data to determine i) a type of anatomical structure and ii) the view of the anatomical structure provided by the schematic representation, based on one or more image features in the first image data, the view being characterized as a geometrically-defined perspective at which the anatomical structure is shown in the schematic representation; accessing (530) second image data being volumetric image data acquired of a patient, the second image data comprising the type of anatomical structure; and generating (540) an output image showing the anatomical structure in the second image data in accordance with said geometrically-defined perspective. 15. A computer-readable medium (600) comprising transitory or non-transitory data (610) representing instructions arranged to cause a processor system to perform the computer-implemented method according to claim 14.
